headline: Visa's Board of Directors has authorized a successive exchange offer for outstanding Class B common stock, contingent upon meeting specific litigation reduction conditions.
investor_impact: The anticipated exchange offer will provide liquidity to Class B-1 and B-2 shareholders by allowing conversion into a mix of restricted Class B-3 and freely transferable Class C stock, which may impact voting dilution and market float. Additionally, the authorization signals management's confidence that unresolved U.S. covered litigation claims will soon fall below the required 50% reduction threshold.
key_figures: 50% or more reduction required in unresolved claims
$49.6 billion estimated interchange reimbursement fees at issue as of October 1, 2023
$39.4 billion estimated interchange reimbursement fees at issue as of October 1, 2025
No. 13-cv-4442 (AKH) 7-Eleven, Inc., et al., v. Visa Inc. litigation case
